paper_title: Climate Model Simulations and Paleoclimate Comparison

methods.description: Describes the configuration of the EC-Earth climate model and the specific forcings and boundary conditions used for the Mid-Holocene and sensitivity experiments.

steps.description: The study is based on a set of simulations performed with the fully coupled global climate model EC-Earth version 3.1.
steps.searchable_summary: Select and use the EC-Earth version 3.1 global climate model.

steps.description: The model is comprised of the Integrated Forecasting System (IFS cycle 36r4) for the atmosphere, the Nucleus for European Modelling of the Ocean version 2 (NEMO2) -for the ocean, and the Louvain-la-Neuve sea-ice Model version 3 (LIM3) for the sea-ice... The IFS model includes the H-TESSEL land surface scheme.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure the EC-Earth model with specified component models (IFS, NEMO2, LIM3, H-TESSEL).

steps.description: The IFS model is run at T159 horizontal spectral resolution corresponding to roughly 1.125° in longitude and latitude, with 62 vertical levels. NEMO has a 1° horizontal resolution except at the equator, where it increases to 1/3°, and 46 vertical levels.
steps.searchable_summary: Set the spatial resolution for the atmospheric (IFS) and oceanic (NEMO) model components.
outputs: Configured EC-Earth model with specified resolution

steps.description: The different components are coupled via the OASIS3 coupler.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure the coupling between model components using OASIS3.

steps.description: Relevant for this study, vegetation cover and monthly aerosol concentrations are prescribed in the model.
steps.searchable_summary: Prescribe vegetation cover and monthly aerosol concentrations as boundary conditions.
outputs: Coupled EC-Earth model with prescribed forcings

steps.description: We analyse an MH experiment (MH PMIP ), which follows the protocol for the standard mid-Holocene simulations in accordance with the third phase of the Paleoclimate Modelling Intercomparison Project (PMIP3)... The MH PMIP includes mid-Holocene orbital forcing and greenhouse gas concentrations, pre-industrial land cover, and airborne dust concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: Define and set up the Mid-Holocene PMIP (MH PMIP) experiment with specified forcings (orbital, GHG, PI land cover, PI dust).

steps.description: In the MH GS ('Green Sahara') setup, the vegetation type (and related parameters, see below) over the Sahara (defined as the area 11° -33°N, 15°W -35°E) is prescribed to be evergreen shrub, representing an idealised African Humid Period scenario, while dust concentration is left unaltered at its pre-industrial (PI) amounts.
steps.searchable_summary: Define and set up the Green Sahara (MH GS) experiment with prescribed evergreen shrub vegetation over the Sahara and PI dust.

steps.description: In the MH RD ('Reduced Dust') setup, the dust concentration over North Africa is reduced by up to 80% relative to pre-industrial values... while the land-surface properties are kept to PI values.
steps.searchable_summary: Define and set up the Reduced Dust (MH RD) experiment with reduced dust concentration over North Africa and PI land surface properties.

steps.description: The final experiment (MH GS+RD ) considers the case where both vegetation and dust changes described above are simultaneously prescribed.
steps.searchable_summary: Define and set up the combined Green Sahara and Reduced Dust (MH GS+RD) experiment with prescribed vegetation changes over Sahara and reduced dust over North Africa.

steps.description: These sensitivity experiments are compared to a PI simulation...
steps.searchable_summary: Note the use of a Pre-Industrial (PI) simulation as a baseline for comparison.

steps.description: The imposed changes in vegetation type correspond to important changes in surface albedo and leaf area index (LAI) as summarised in Table 2. Under the Green Sahara scenario, the albedo decreases from 0.3 to 0.15, while the LAI increases from 0.2 to 2.6.
steps.searchable_summary: Note the specific changes in surface albedo and LAI associated with the Green Sahara vegetation change.

methods.method_name: Climate Model Data Analysis and Comparison
methods.description: Analysis of the output from the EC-Earth simulations, focusing on specific time periods and seasons, comparing results between different experiments, and evaluating the statistical significance of the differences.

steps.description: The analysis focuses on the June-September (JJAS) period using the last 50 years of each experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract data for the last 50 years and the June-September (JJAS) season from each simulation output.
inputs: Output data from MH PMIP, MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD, PI simulations
outputs: Subsetted data (last 50 years, JJAS) for each simulation

steps.description: These sensitivity experiments are compared to a PI simulation to investigate the role of each forcing in altering the SAM.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare the results of the sensitivity experiments (MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD) with the Pre-Industrial (PI) simulation results.
inputs: Subsetted data from MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD, PI simulations
outputs: Differences/anomalies between sensitivity experiments and PI

steps.description: We focus on the most apparent dissimilarities between the sensitivity experiments and the standard MH simulation (MH PMIP ) where only orbital forcing is considered.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare the results of the sensitivity experiments (MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD) with the standard Mid-Holocene PMIP (MH PMIP) simulation results.
inputs: Subsetted data from MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD, MH PMIP simulations
outputs: Differences/dissimilarities between sensitivity experiments and MH PMIP

steps.description: Finally, the statistical significance of the differences between experiments at the 5% level is evaluated by a two-tailed Student's t test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a two-tailed Student's t test to evaluate the statistical significance of the differences between simulation experiments.

methods.description: Comparison of climate model simulation outputs, particularly precipitation patterns, with available paleoclimate proxy records from marine and terrestrial archives to evaluate model performance.

steps.description: To evaluate the model performance when accounting for Sahara greening and reduction in airborne dust concentrations, we compare our simulations to the available marine and terrestrial paleoclimate archives.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain data or interpretations from relevant marine and terrestrial paleoclimate proxy archives for the early-middle Holocene (~6000 years BP).
inputs: List of relevant paleoclimate proxy sites/studies (e.g., Nal Sarovar, Thar desert records, Prasad et al., 1997; Deotare et al., 2004; Gill et al., 2015, 2017)
outputs: Paleoclimate proxy data/interpretations (e.g., precipitation estimates, lake levels)

steps.description: We focus on the most apparent dissimilarities between the sensitivity experiments and the standard MH simulation (MH PMIP )... Notable differences in summer precipitation between the four simulations occur over western India (Fig. 2), which shows substantially wetter conditions in MH GS and MH GS+RD , compared to the MH PMIP experiment in that region.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze and visualize simulation outputs, focusing on key variables like summer precipitation, and identify differences between experiments, particularly over regions with proxy data (e.g., western India).
inputs: Subsetted simulation data (last 50 years, JJAS) for MH PMIP, MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD
outputs: Analyzed simulation results (e.g., precipitation maps, difference maps)

steps.description: To evaluate the model performance... we compare our simulations to the available marine and terrestrial paleoclimate archives. ... Compare simulation outputs (e.g., summer precipitation patterns from MH PMIP, MH GS, MH RD, MH GS+RD) with the paleoclimate proxy data.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare the analyzed simulation results (e.g., simulated precipitation changes) with the paleoclimate proxy data/interpretations.
inputs: Analyzed simulation results (e.g., simulated precipitation changes)
outputs: Comparison results (agreement/disagreement between model and proxy)

steps.description: This suggests that the modern teleconnections may not precisely hold in the past and the inferred changes based on only tropical Pacific SST patterns may underestimate the total rainfall changes during the early and middle Holocene over north-western India.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret the comparison results, considering potential reasons for agreement or disagreement between model and proxy data (e.g., validity of modern teleconnections in the past).
